item.title: Not being organized is a luxury we can no longer afford
item.description: Going to voting booths is important but can not be all. You have to organize, more than ever! Is there a DSA Chapter near by you? A ICE Rapid response Team (ICErr.com) or some folk who organized No-King-Rallies or a food-no-bombs group? JOIN THEM! You think you are too old? Doesn't matter at all, as long you respect the youngsters. You think you have not enough money or time to organize. You will be surprise how supportive people around you will impact your live. You think about yourself as an asshole and doesn't want to hurt people. Be sure they will tell you when you go to far. Classic Social Anxiety or simple introverted? I can not say how often I heard from people with this Disorder how comfortable they feet in political groups, how different it was from anything they had known before. You have not the knowledge about politics? They will talk about it and talk about it and talk about it and ... BTW, also a great place to train your abilities to say "No" and "Stop". You are thinking about yourself that you can't contribute anything to the group? Wrong. Did I miss anything?
item.dc:creator: onushka

item.title: Israel struck Iran's largest natural gas processing plant in a coordinated operation with the US. Two senior Israeli officials told Axios the strike was carried out with US approval. – Trump claiming the US "knew nothing" about the strike
item.description:
Israel attacked the gas processing plant 'Asaluyeh' in the city of Bushehr in southwestern Iran. It is considered the largest and most important gas facility in Iran. Almost 80% of Iranian electricity is generated from natural gas, and half of it comes from "South Pars".
BREAKING NEWS: Iran strikes Ras Laffan refinery in Qatar, triggering a fire at one of the world's largest LNG and gas processing hubs.
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: Memes vol 643
item.description:
“Waaaa she probably thinks she’s better than the people who use AI” yes I do. Use your brain. I think I died in the inside when my coworker told me she used ChatGPT to wrote her mom’s eulogy.
So yeah this mindset hasn’t happened to me yet
I’ve never seen love island but I have to imagine this would be hilarious
So Lyonel preferred men, right?
I used to work with someone who found her cat outside in a dumpster and said he was part Maine coon… chat when I tell you this cat was a fat domestic long hair. So obviously Big Mama is a Maine coon because she’s polydactyl, right?
This is insane they’re identical
Gertie tax
Baby Bruce tax
Nessie tax
Big Mama tax

item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: GovBrief Today #412
item.description: The Pentagon today asked the White House for $200 billion to continue a war that neither of them has the constitutional authority to declare. The Constitution assigns only Congress the power to declare war. Three weeks into bombing Iran, they've abandoned it for the third time. Wednesday's war powers resolution failed 53-47 after Republicans voted en masse to kill it. The official who oversees all 18 American intelligence agencies then told senators that only the president could determine whether Iran was an imminent threat before the attack. Her own counterterrorism chief resigned the day before, saying it was not. The Treasury Department confirmed Wednesday that the national debt crossed $39 trillion, the same day the Pentagon asked for the money to keep bombing the third country we've attacked since December. Call your senators and remind them they swore an oath to the document that puts war authority in their hands. Demand they honor it. We've emailed 6,600+ stories over 412 straight nights. The algorithm doesn't always show them to you. Subscribe now at govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Today on the Website: https://go.govbrief.today/260318-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/nebraska-wildfires [2] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-mar18 [3] https://go.govbrief.today/epstein-walkout [4] https://go.govbrief.today/fed-march26 [5] https://go.govbrief.today/debt-accelerates [6] https://go.govbrief.today/oil-pressure [7] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-supplemental [8] https://go.govbrief.today/second-honors [9] https://go.govbrief.today/gabbard-iran [10] https://go.govbrief.today/wi-attorney-bondi [11] https://go.govbrief.today/fbi-data [12] https://go.govbrief.today/contreras-released [13] https://go.govbrief.today/mullin-nom-hearing [14] https://go.govbrief.today/gop-war-vote [15] https://go.govbrief.today/radar-separation [16] https://go.govbrief.today/rodney-statue [17] https://go.govbrief.today/duffy-sil [18] https://go.govbrief.today/harris-scotus Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Art by Tolkien – The Lord of the Rings
item.description:
This is an account on illustrations by the author of places in The Lord of the Rings, including a few pieces he made for The Hobbit which are relevant for both tales.
Beside being an accomplished philologist and writer, J.R.R. Tolkien was a painter. He rarely portrayed people but left us with sketches and paintings of landscapes and settlements, to which I link associated lore posts for those who feel curious;
3. The Hill, home to the Bagginses in the village of Hobbiton in the Shire The Shire geography: https://imgur.com/gallery/vOpC3EF
4. The Hall at Bag End, residence of Bilbo and later also Frodo Baggins Hobbit homes: https://imgur.com/gallery/xLlk0NN
5. Old Man Willow in the Old Forest, east of the Shire Awake trees: https://imgur.com/gallery/O3zwZfM
6. Looking east toward Rivendell, also known as Imladris
7. Another version of the vale of Imladris History: https://imgur.com/gallery/iv5lFmS
8. The tower of Orthanc in Isengard
9. Later Orthanc sketch
10. Orthanc in Isengard History: https://imgur.com/gallery/Jgz2a1W
11. The western door of Moria, with a tentacle of the Watcher seen in the lake The Watcher: https://imgur.com/gallery/oTXA6hW
12. Lothlórien in Spring (with golden mellorn trees) Forest names: https://imgur.com/gallery/oEVQ4SO
13. The Hornburg at Helm's Deep, in Rohan History: https://imgur.com/gallery/mgAPTMe
14. The refuge of Dunharrow, leading to the Paths of the Dead, in Rohan The undead Oathbreakers: https://imgur.com/gallery/qbfrNJF
15. Minas Tirith in Gondor (unfinished and notably not yet having a vast pier of rock)
16. Minas Tirith sketch History: https://imgur.com/gallery/k3tbj2H
17. The Stairs of Cirith Ungol to Shelob's lair (before the Tower of Cirith Ungol, Mordor) The kin of Shelob: https://imgur.com/gallery/mtfdhCJ
18. Barad-dûr, the Dark Tower in Mordor, with Mount Doom in the background History: https://imgur.com/gallery/qUtRavu
19. The holy mountain Taniquetil in Valinor, the Blessed Realm across the western Sea, that we do not see but the Ringbearers would at the end of the tale Mortals in the Blessed Realm: https://imgur.com/gallery/C6GSVN7
For more Tolkien lore posts, enjoy these two indexes: Character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF Folks, events, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL
item.dc:creator: Ngugi
